children.heading: Grants
children.label: 1 Grants
children.text: On the application of an eligible recipient, the Secretary may make grants for training, research, and technical assistance, including grants for program evaluation and economic impact analyses, that would be useful in alleviating or preventing conditions of excessive unemployment or underemployment.

children.label: A
children.text: project planning, project predevelopment, and feasibility studies;

children.label: B
children.text: demonstrations of innovative activities or strategic economic development investments;

children.label: C
children.text: management and operational assistance;

children.label: D
children.text: establishment of university centers;

children.label: E
children.text: establishment of business outreach centers;

children.label: F
children.text: studies evaluating the needs of, and development potential for, economic growth of areas that the Secretary determines have substantial need for the assistance;

children.label: G
children.text: studies that evaluate the effectiveness of coordinating projects funded under this chapter with projects funded under other Acts;

children.label: H
children.text: assessment, marketing, and establishment of business clusters; and

children.label: I
children.text: other activities determined by the Secretary to be appropriate.

children.heading: Cooperation requirement
children.label: 3 Cooperation requirement
children.text: In the case of a project assisted under this section that is national or regional in scope, the Secretary may waive the provision in requiring a nonprofit organization or association to act in cooperation with officials of a political subdivision of a State.section 3122(6)(A)(vi) of this title

children.heading: Methods of provision of assistance
children.label: b Methods of provision of assistance

children.text: provide research and technical assistance through officers or employees of the Department;
pay funds made available to carry out this section to Federal agencies; or
employ private individuals, partnerships, businesses, corporations, or appropriate institutions under contracts entered into for that purpose.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: In accordance with subsection (a)(2)(D), the Secretary may make grants to institutions of higher education to serve as university centers.

children.heading: Geographic coverage
children.label: 2 Geographic coverage
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that the network of university centers established under this subsection provides services in each State.

children.label: A
children.text: collaborate with other university centers;

children.label: B
children.text: collaborate with economic development districts and other relevant Federal economic development technical assistance and service providers to provide expertise and technical assistance to develop, implement, and support comprehensive economic development strategies and other economic development planning at the local, regional, and State levels, with a focus on innovation, entrepreneurship, workforce development, and regional economic development;

children.label: C
children.text: provide technical assistance, business development, and technology transfer services to businesses in the area served by the university center;

children.label: D
children.text: establish partnerships with 1 or more commercialization intermediaries that are public or nonprofit technology transfer organizations eligible to receive a grant under ;section 1862s–9 of this title

children.label: E
children.text: promote local and regional capacity building; and

children.label: F
children.text: provide to communities and regions assistance relating to data collection and analysis and other research relating to economic conditions and vulnerabilities that can inform economic development and adjustment strategies.

children.label: A
children.text: the significant role of regional public universities in supporting economic development in distressed communities through the planning and the implementation of economic development projects and initiatives; and

children.label: B
children.text: the location of the university center in or near a distressed community.
